action.reasoning: Observation: all surviving full fine-tuned checkpoints now validate at ~0.19 overall / ~0.15 worst locally, suggesting the strong submitted checkpoint was overwritten or current usable files are corrupted/collapsed. Analysis: with only 2 iterations left, the safest recoverable path is to build a fresh offline CLIP-L frozen-feature classifier: extract CLIP vision pooler features once for train/val, train a small MLP head with class/region-aware weighting, select by OOD-val worst-region, and save a lightweight checkpoint that predict.py can load with pretrained CLIP from the local HF cache. This avoids risky full-backbone fine-tuning and should produce a valid non-collapsed model; even if it does not beat the earlier framework best, submitting it will not erase the recorded best score. Decision: patch model.py/predict.py for linear/MLP head checkpoints, add train_linear.py, train feature-head model, then submit the selected checkpoint.
